REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS MARGARET TAKAHASHI, MASASHI TAKAHASHI'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, BREACH OF CONTRACT, SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE AND NEGLIGENCE. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. VERACITY OF THE ALLEGATIONS CANNOT BE PROPERLY ADDRESSED ON A DEMURRER. DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS GIVING RISE TO FIDUCIARY DUTY OWED BY DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF AND BREACH OF SUCH DUTY. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE ALLEGED DAMAGE RESULTED FROM THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION OR TO ALLEGE ANY OTHER DAMAGED CAUSED BY SUCH MISREPRESENTATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ANNE CHRISTINE MASSULLO, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS MARGARET TAKAHASHI, MASASHI TAKAHASHI'S AMENDED MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM ORDER AND RECONSIDERATION DUE TO ATTORNEY FAULT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN. AS TO CCP 473(B) NO AUTHORITY IS CITED FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT RELIEF IS AVAILABLE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES AND NO INDICATION (BRIEF, OUTLINE OF ARGUMENT) OF WHAT MOVING PARTY WOULD HAVE ARGUED HAD HE COMPLIED WITH THE TENTATIVE RULING RULE. MOTION UNDER CCP 1008 IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS IT IS NOT BROUGHT ON PROPER BASIS, I.E. "ATTORNEY FAULT" AND "MISTAKE" ARE NOT GROUNDS FOR THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Order And Reconsideration Due To Attorney Fault; Memorandum In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARGARET TAKAHASHI, MASASHI TAKAHASHI'S AMENDED MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM ORDER AND RECONSIDERATION DUE TO ATTORNEY FAULT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party fails to sustain its burden. As to CCP 473(b) no authority is cited for the proposition that relief is available under the circumstances and no indication (brief, outline of argument) of what moving party would have argued had he complied with the tentative ruling rule. Motion under CCP 1008 is denied without prejudice as it is not brought on proper basis, i.e. "attorney fault" and "mistake" are not grounds for the motion for reconsideration. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Moot. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANTS PATRICIA KILLEN, SEAN KILLEN, JOSEPH NOBLE, JOHN KILLEN, STEAM LABS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Cross-Complainant to allege facts (not conclusions) to support each cause of action as to each cross-defendant. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARGARET TAKAHASHI, MASASHI TAKAHASHI'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Carlton D. Floyd of the Law Office of Bradford C. Floyd (707.445.9754) appearing via Court Call for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.

Mr. Floyd informs the Court that he failed to notify opposing counsel of his intent to contest the tentative ruling. The Court does not hear arguments and adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for declaratory relief, breach of contract, specific performance and negligence. These causes of action are sufficiently alleged. Veracity of the allegations cannot be properly addressed on a demurrer. Demurrer to cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to fiduciary duty owed by defendant to plaintiff and breach of such duty. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action for fraud and negligent misrepresentation for Plaintiff to allege facts showing that the alleged damage resulted from the alleged misrepresentation or to allege any other damaged caused by such misrepresentation. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Anne Christine Massullo, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/ACM)

AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARGARET TAKAHASHI, MASASHI TAKAHASHI'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Carlton D. Floyd of the Law Office of Bradford C. Floyd (707.445.9754) appearing via Court Call for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.

Mr. Floyd informs the Court that he failed to notify opposing counsel of his intent to contest the tentative ruling. The Court does not hear arguments and adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burned under CCP 425.16. While Defendant points to factual allegations in the complaint which constitute protected activity, no claim asserted in any of the seven causes of action "arises out of" the protected activity. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Anne Christine Massullo, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/ACM)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-06-2018 Off Calendar

Defendants Margaret Takahashi And Masashi Takahashi'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Carlton D. Floyd, esq. (707-445-9754) for Defendants/opposing party Margaret Takahashi And Masashi Takahashi via Court Call.
No other appearances.

Compliance with Tentative Ruling Rule. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Margaret Takahashi And Masashi Takahashi'S Demurrer To Complaint is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to address all issues raised by demurrer. Plaintiff failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B and Court's January 4, 2018 minute order to provide courtesy copy of opposition. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANTS MARGARET TAKAHASHI and MASASHI TAKAHASHI's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is transferred and continued to be heard on January 18, 2018 at 9:30 AM in department 501, the real property/housing court. The parties are required to provide courtesy copies of the complaint and the papers they filed on the demurrer no later than January 5, 2018. Department 501 hears all cases involving commercial lease disputes and all further law and motion matters in this case must be noticed to be heard in that department. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.
